channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.mmorpg.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.mmorpg.com

item.title: Pantheon: Rise of the Fallen Previews More Combat & Progression Update Features, From Engaging NPCs to Items
item.content:encoded:
Pantheon: Rise of the Fallen’s Combat and Progression Update is coming soon. Visionary Realms has released tons of info on class reworks, Masteries, combat, NPC changes, and even a new zone. This time, the team is detailing some of the other features and additional changes we can expect (and even a little PTR hint).

Right away at the beginning of this dev diary, they dive into the question of when the PTR is coming. The answer is that they’re “in the final stages of testing the infrastructure that you will use to connect to that server”, but there are a number of other parts of the foundation they still need to solidify. So, no date just yet.

As for the other parts of the update, one of the changes will be a “First to Engage” system for kill credit and looting rights. If you’re groaning, the devs are already working on ways to try and mitigate some of the potential frustration this sort of system brings.

NPCs will spawn with a brief delay before they can be attacked or engaged, even with AoEs. This window should give everyone in range a chance to tag that NPC. There’s also going to be something to prevent someone from just tagging and kiting an NPC endlessly.

NPC leashing is also going to get an overhaul to be more dynamic and scale appropriately in different situations. Examples include a wolf chasing you until you leave its territory, or a bandit  chasing you until you get far enough to break the line of sight. 

As part of itemization and economic changes, most items will be Bind on Equip in an effort to keep rare and special loot special and from  lingering around the economy and affecting it by remaining in circulation. We'll also see clearer item tooltips as well as color coding for rarity. 

Another feature that is coming to help everyone is a rested experience bonus. You can rested XP when logged out and when you log back in, there will be a slight temporary experience gain bonus.

The team also lists a number of other pieces on the way, including hotbar simplification, spell pushback, abilities that can have multiple uses, bane damage on items, and a Charm mechanic. This latter lets characters with certain abilities, and at certain risk, temporarily take control of NPCs. We think that one deserves more of a preview when the time comes.



https://www.mmorpg.com/news/pantheon-rise-of-the-fallen-previews-more-combat-and-progression-update-features-from-engaging-npcs-to-items-2000137906
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
